intro.title: From Çaldıran to the Pyramids
intro.body: Selim the Grim defeated the Safavids at Çaldıran in 1514, then turned south to end Mamluk rule. Marj Dabiq broke the Mamluk field army in 1516; Ridaniyya delivered Cairo in 1517. Selim took the title of Servant of the Two Holy Sanctuaries.

stops.name: Istanbul
stops.note: Selim's working capital. The Topkapı palace is then sixty years old.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Topkapı palace gardens with Janissaries assembling in the second courtyard, Bosphorus in distance.

stops.name: Çaldıran
stops.note: 23 August 1514. Selim's Janissary arquebuses and field artillery break Shah Ismail's cavalry charge on the plateau east of Lake Van.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. High plateau battle, line of wagons with cannon, Safavid cavalry breaking against musket smoke. army camp, banners on the steppe.

stops.name: Marj Dabiq
stops.note: 24 August 1516. Selim defeats and kills the Mamluk sultan Qansuh al-Ghuri north of Aleppo. The Levant is opened to the Ottomans.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Syrian plain with two armies opposed, Mamluk heavy cavalry against Ottoman gunpowder line, banner of a fallen sultan in the foreground. army camp, banners.

stops.name: Jerusalem
stops.note: December 1516. Selim enters Jerusalem on his way south; receives the keys of the Haram al-Sharif from the city's notables.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Damascus Gate of Jerusalem, sultan on horseback receiving keys on a velvet cushion.

stops.name: Cairo (Ridaniyya)
stops.note: 22 January 1517. Selim's flanking march around the Mamluk fortified line at Ridaniyya wins the battle. Tuman Bay is hanged at the Bab Zuwayla weeks later.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Cairo skyline with pyramids in the haze beyond, Mamluk fortified positions being outflanked by Janissary infantry.
